mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, reinforcing earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 45%) or shows negative correlation with SPX.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and fading economic momentum, causing equity valuations to compress and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction in easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the consumer price index below the 235.0 threshold removes disinflationary pressure on central bank policy, prompting markets to anticipate rate cuts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL falls below 235.0, or the threshold effect disappears in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical fluctuation, depressing corporate revenue forecasts and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns (win rate < 40%) or shows positive correlation with SPX during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate profit pools and validates economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across multiple expansion cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signals demand destruction and inventory overhang, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in INDPRO and RSAFS during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power outside late-cycle regimes.
